text:
Sundar Pichai, CEO of Google and Alphabet Inc. and a Stanford alum, will return to the Farm to deliver Stanford’s 135th Commencement address.

After earning a master's degree in materials science and engineering at Stanford, Pichai went on to help build and lead products used by billions around the world, including Google Chrome. He will address graduates and their guests at Stanford Stadium on June 14. The ceremony will be Pichai’s first in-person commencement speech.

“Sundar is a deeply thoughtful leader who has a vivid sense for how technology can improve society and transform individual lives. He epitomizes what it means for a Stanford graduate to have a broad impact,” said Stanford President Jon Levin. “I'm excited to hear his reflections and advice to our graduates as they take the next step into their own futures.”

Excited to see Google ranked at the top of Fast Company's Most Innovative Companies, it’s not something I or any of us at Google take for granted. It’s been a lot of work over a long period of time.

So much dynamism is happening in technology right now, and we have to work hard every day to earn our success and deliver incredible products for users and partners. But I’m feeling especially proud of our teams today. 

As I told Fast Company there will be plenty of uncomfortably exciting times, but we aren’t slowing down! 

https://lnkd.in/gSisNM2g

We’re introducing new Gemini capabilities across Google Docs, Sheets, Slides and Drive to help you get things done faster. 

You can now allow Gemini to pull relevant information from your files, emails and the web, to connect dots and uncover useful insights.

Here’s what else you can do:

📑 Docs: Go from a blank page to a fully formatted first draft instantly by drawing information from your own files. Plus, use "Match Writing Style" so it doesn’t sound like the doc was edited by committee, even if it was :)

🔢 Sheets: Describe what you need and Gemini will build entire spreadsheets, solve complex problems, and fill in the data 9X faster than if you had to manually enter it yourself. Gemini in Sheets is now #1 on SheetsBench full leaderboard. 

🎤 Slides: Generate professional, on-brand layouts and editable diagrams from a simple prompt, no design skills required!

💽 Drive: No more digging through folders to find what you’re looking for. AI Overviews give you summarized, cited answers at the top of your search results (I love this!).

These new features are starting to roll out today in beta, available first to Google AI Ultra and Pro subscribers in English globally for Google Docs, Sheets and Slides, and in the US for Drive. 

See everything that’s coming here: https://lnkd.in/gjcjjGgE

Returning to India for the India AI Impact Summit 2026 last week, I was struck by the incredible energy in New Delhi. There is a real sense that the world has moved beyond just talking about the promise of AI, to how it can meaningfully improve people’s lives at scale.

I’ve been reflecting on this progress in the context of my own life.

Back when I was a student, I often took the Coromandel Express train from Chennai up to IIT Kharagpur. To get there we passed through Visakhapatnam—Vizag. I remember it being a quiet and modest coastal city, brimming with potential.  Now, in that same city, Google is establishing a full-stack AI hub, part of our $15 billion infrastructure investment in India. When finished, this hub will house gigawatt-scale compute and a new international subsea cable gateway, bringing jobs and cutting-edge AI to people and businesses across India.

Sitting on that train, I never imagined Vizag becoming a global AI hub. India is on an incredible trajectory with AI, and we want to continue to be a partner. To support that progress last week we launched the America-India Connect initiative, new subsea cable routes to boost connectivity between the US, India, and many locations in the Southern Hemisphere. We also introduced new Google DeepMind partnerships to broaden access to our frontier AI capabilities and our most ambitious skilling programs to date.

It was a week of exciting conversations with world leaders, partners, students and Googlers. I’m more convinced than ever that we can work together to realize AI’s full benefits. 

I even managed to get in a bit of cricket practice between sessions (with some expert advice too from Shubman!). 

Thank you India for hosting this incredible summit!

Our Q4/FY’25 results are in. Thanks to our partners & employees, it was a tremendous quarter, exceeding $400B in annual revenue for the first time. 

Our full AI stack is fueling our progress, and Gemini 3 adoption has been faster than any other model in our history. Meanwhile, Gemini App has grown to 750M monthly active users, seeing significantly higher engagement per user, especially since the launch of Gemini 3 in December. 

Some other highlights:

👉 Search continued to accelerate with revenues growing 17% year-over-year. We saw more Search usage in Q4 than ever before, as AI continues to drive an expansionary moment. Gemini 3 is now integrated into AI Mode in Search and AI Overviews, and we’ve made the transition from an AI Overview to a conversation in AI Mode completely seamless.

👉 YouTube’s annual revenues surpassed $60B across ads and subscriptions. It continues to be #1 in streaming the US, according to Nielsen. Strong adoption of YouTube Premium, along with Google One, has driven more than 325M paid subscriptions across consumer services.

👉 Cloud revenues grew 48% in Q4, now on an annual run rate of $70B+. Backlog grew by 55% quarter over quarter to $240B, representing a wide breadth of customers and driven by demand for our AI products. That includes Gemini Enterprise, launched in October, which has sold more than 8M paid seats.

👉 And finally, Waymo raised its largest investment round to date this week. In December we surpassed 20M fully autonomous trips, with 400K+ rides provided every week, and its sixth market, Miami, launched two weeks ago, with plans to expand to multiple more cities. 

In closing, 2025 was a fantastic year for the company. We’re really well positioned going into 2026, and excited for what’s ahead! Read my full remarks: https://lnkd.in/gGYV4B_e
